acceptance_targets: [CH13_CRAFT_SCHEMATIC_FALLOUT_QUEUE] Pass only if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:55; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:125; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:151; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:203 gives each major exchange a visible consequence before the next one begins: a character changes location or allegiance on page, Jun-seo's behavior materially worsens or redirects, or Noor makes and fails or refuses a concrete intervention. Fail if multiple accusation scenes still end with the accuser leaving and Noor returning to the same passive corridor watch state.

[CH13_DIALOGUE_PRIYA_CALIBRATION_ECHO] Pass only if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:179; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:183; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:185 does not reproduce the sequence 'my mother' plus treatment or chemotherapy plus 'can't/cannot send anything back,' and if Priya reaches the family or visa stakes through at least one character-true deflection, self-correction, or interruption before she arrives at the accusation. Fail if the scene still hinges on a polished single-sentence moral summary or on calibration-echo phrasing.

[CH13_PROSE_OPAQUE_CORPSE_LEDGER] Pass only if the revised beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 either states the jump to nineteen without listing opaque identifiers, or names only dead who are immediately legible from the revised passage and attaches each to a concrete image or consequence. Fail if the paragraph still relies on shorthand entries such as anonymous positional labels to carry its emotional weight.
[RED-018] Ch13 contains at most one brief cold reference, not two separate re-proofs.
[RED-019] Ch13 does not describe Marcus's skin color using any gray/yellow compound.
[RED-037] 'still' appears no more than 10 times in chapter 13.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:55; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:125; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:151; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:203 gives each major exchange a visible consequence before the next one begins: a character changes location or allegiance on page, Jun-seo's behavior materially worsens or redirects, or Noor makes and fails or refuses a concrete intervention. Fail if multiple accusation scenes still end with the accuser leaving and Noor returning to the same passive corridor watch state.
findings.problem: The middle third announces a reaction queue and then executes it as four mostly isolated witness visits plus repeated Jun-seo-watch beats. Because these exchanges usually end with the speaker leaving and Noor returning to the same corridor wait state, the humiliation starts reading like required fallout being checked off rather than a socially unstable scene that keeps changing shape. The chapter still reaches Ahmad's killing, but too much of the runway is spent certifying points the reader already has: some blame Noor, some do not care, and Jun-seo is unraveling.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:55; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:61; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:93; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:125; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:151; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:199 so at least one confrontation collides with another pressure already in motion. Options are to merge or cut one standalone accusation visit, let Noor attempt a concrete intervention with Jun-seo that fails, or make one exchange immediately alter seating, corridor access, or who is willing to stay near whom. Keep the must-land beats, but do not keep the current pattern where each beat arrives, states its thesis, and clears the stage for the next visitor.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:179; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:183; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:185 does not reproduce the sequence 'my mother' plus treatment or chemotherapy plus 'can't/cannot send anything back,' and if Priya reaches the family or visa stakes through at least one character-true deflection, self-correction, or interruption before she arrives at the accusation. Fail if the scene still hinges on a polished single-sentence moral summary or on calibration-echo phrasing.
findings.problem: Priya's confrontation echoes calibration-only style-bible language too closely and lands in overly composed, thesis-clean beats. The move from dissertation compromise to the mother/visa disclosure to 'I needed one person in this building whose judgment was not compromised' reads more like a polished moral set-piece than the indirect, formal-leaning voice that should tighten and then shatter under this kind of pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revoice snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:157; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:179; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:183; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:185 so Priya reaches her family and visa stakes through character-specific syntax instead of the current calibration-adjacent ladder. Preserve the facts, but route them through an indirect opening, at least one self-correction or interrupted clause, and a messier accusation shape. Avoid the current 'my mother ... chemotherapy ... cannot send anything back' progression and flatten the quote-card neatness of the judgment line.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:239 either states the jump to nineteen without listing opaque identifiers, or names only dead who are immediately legible from the revised passage and attaches each to a concrete image or consequence. Fail if the paragraph still relies on shorthand entries such as anonymous positional labels to carry its emotional weight.
findings.problem: Once Ahmad's death is already concrete on the page, the chapter pauses for a mixed named/unnamed corpse inventory that reads like bookkeeping. Several items in the tally are only shorthand labels rather than immediately legible presences, so the line asks the reader to decode old deaths instead of deepening Noor's immediate horror. That blunts the transition from Ahmad's body to Ember's next message.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:237; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:239; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_13.md:245. Either compress the recognition to a sharper realization that the count is now nineteen, or turn the count into a brief set of vivid, immediately legible memory flashes anchored in Noor's present sensory field. Do not keep the current ledger of partially unidentified dead between Ahmad's body and Ember's chime.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 contains at most one brief cold reference, not two separate re-proofs.
findings.evidence: Ch12 L3392: 'Air pushes through the ceiling vent, colder than it was this morning'; Ch13 L3549: 'Cold has pushed through the fabric at her knees'; Ch13 L3651: 'Cold from the floor works through Noor's shoes.'
findings.problem: Ch13 contains two incidental cold re-proofs (L3549, L3651) that add no new information about the temperature drop. Part of the Ch10-13 cluster (7 cold re-proofs across 4 chapters without material escalation).
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut one or both cold references. The cold is ambient by Ch13; let it live in the reader's assumption rather than re-proving it through fabric and shoes.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 does not describe Marcus's skin color using any gray/yellow compound.
findings.evidence: Ch12 L3168: 'his skin has the gray-yellow of a person whose organs are shutting down by committee'; Ch13 L3662: 'The skin around his eyes has gone the bruised yellow-gray of old fruit.'
findings.problem: Marcus's gray-yellow skin is re-described in adjacent chapters with near-synonymous compound color descriptors. The 'gray-yellow'/'yellow-gray' phrasing repeats the same deterioration signal without new medical information. This is the third time since Ch8 L2007 ('His skin has gone gray').
findings.rewrite_direction: At Ch13 L3662, describe Marcus through a different physical channel (sound of breathing, posture, smell) rather than repeating the skin-color observation.

findings.acceptance_test: 'still' appears no more than 10 times in chapter 13.
findings.evidence: 'still' appears ~22 times in Ch13 (~3,470 prose words = 1 per 158 words). Manuscript-wide: 321 occurrences / 75,542 prose words = 1 per 235 words, which is 3.4x the 1/800 threshold.
findings.problem: Ch13 has the worst 'still' density in the manuscript (1 per 158 words, 5.1x threshold). 'Still' is the single most overused qualifier: 321 total occurrences. It creates a pervasive verbal tic suggesting stasis/continuation where the prose should convey forward motion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 12 of the ~22 'still' instances in Ch13. In most cases, the sentence works without the word: 'the server room is locked' instead of 'the server room is still locked.' Where temporal persistence genuinely matters, replace with 'yet,' 'continues,' or restructure.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
